name: Raspberry Chocolate Truffle Pie
recipeIngredient: 15 ounce pkg. Pillsbury All-Ready Pie Crusts
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F.
2. Prepare pie crust according to package directions for an unfilled one-crust pie using a 9-inch pie pan. Refrigerate the remaining crust for later use.
3. Bake at 450°F for 9-11 minutes or until light golden brown. Allow to cool completely.
4. To prepare sauce, press 4 cups raspberries through a strainer to make 1 cup puree. Discard seeds. In a small saucepan, combine sugar and cream of tartar; blend well. Stir in puree; mix well. Over medium heat, bring the mixture to a boil. Boil for 3 minutes, stirring constantly. Cover and refrigerate.
5. In a medium heavy saucepan, combine sweetened condensed milk and 2/3 cup whipping cream; blend well. Over medium heat, bring the mixture to a boil. Remove from heat and let cool for 5 minutes. In a food processor bowl with a metal blade, combine the milk mixture and melted chocolate; process for 5-10 seconds.
6. Add egg yolks; process for 5 seconds. Add raspberry-flavored liqueur and amaretto; process for 5 seconds. Pour the blended mixture into a large bowl and set over ice. Stir for 5 to 7 minutes or until the mixture is cool. Beat on high speed for 2-3 minutes or until soft peaks form. Pour into the cooled baked crust. Refrigerate about 1 hour or until firm.
7. In a small bowl, beat 1 1/3 cups whipping cream until soft peaks form. Add confectioner's sugar and vanilla; beat until stiff peaks form. Spread over the cooled filling. Garnish with chocolate curls and whole raspberries, if desired. Refrigerate until serving time. Remove from refrigerator 1 hour before serving. Serve with raspberry sauce. Store in refrigerator.
Makes 10-12 servings.
One teaspoon raspberry extract plus enough water to measure 2 tablespoons may be substituted for raspberry liqueur.
One teaspoon almond extract plus enough water to measure 2 tablespoons can be substituted for amaretto.
